text: The surgeon uses a robotic-assisted approach to repair the large intercostal hernia. Minimal dissection is performed to create space between the right colon, right liver, and abdominal wall. A large piece of coated polypropylene mesh is inserted and oriented within the abdomen, with several stay sutures placed circumferentially to anchor the mesh to the abdominal wall.

The surgeon uses a running self-retaining absorbable suture to secure the mesh circumferentially, starting with the posterior location. The mesh is sewn completely around, and additional anchoring sutures are placed to prevent displacement or migration.
